subject: Robot HC CLI port setting
commit_message:
Robot HC CLI port setting

Issue-ID: CLI-261

Signed-off-by: Kanagaraj Manickam <kanagaraj.manickam@huawei.com>
Change-Id: I11ea69bf16f5e57b680c7a8260453c893a4bdd02

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

For information the list of the other current HTTPS ports defined in Robot (Master OOM deployment)

GLOBAL_AAI_SERVER_PORT = "8443"
GLOBAL_APPC_SERVER_PORT = "8443"
GLOBAL_SDC_FE_PORT = "9443"
GLOBAL_SDC_BE_PORT = "8443"
GLOBAL_CLAMP_SERVER_PORT = "8443"
GLOBAL_NBI_SERVER_PORT = "8443"
GLOBAL_CLI_SERVER_PORT = "8443"
GLOBAL_DMAAP_DR_PROV_SERVER_PORT = "443"
GLOBAL_DMAAP_DR_NODE_SERVER_PORT = "8443"
GLOBAL_MSB_SERVER_PORT = "443"
GLOBAL_BC_HTTPS_SERVER_PORT = "8443"
GLOBAL_DEPLOYMENT_HANDLER_SERVER_PORT = "8443"
GLOBAL_PORTAL_SERVER_PORT = "8443"
GLOBAL_SDNC_REST_PORT = "8443"
GLOBAL_SMS_SERVER_PORT = "10443"
GLOBAL_VID_SERVER_PORT = '8443'

Patch Set 1: Verified-1 Code-Review-2

ports go in the properties that come from oom. Add to this file: https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=testsuite/oom.git;a=blob;f=resources/config/eteshare/config/robot_properties.py;h=974a996bd6db8d89e55b56ee64f13d35bfc4787f;hb=refs/heads/master

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Verified+1 Code-Review+2
> 
> it is just to align the repo and oom/testsuite (where the change has already been done)
> @Daniel I assume you can remove your -2, this patch shall be harmless

since this property is already defined in the oom properties file, it does not need to be defined here. No other ports are here, and it could cause confusion if people start overriding ports here (when you would expect the value to be in the property file in the --variable argument from ete-k8s.sh.

This patch should be abandoned.

>

I have no objection to abandon the patch
but should we not remove the line then, because it remains misleading if we have 2 files with the same var and different values, no?
